description: A loose grouping of early-20th-century organized crime gangs formed among Italian immigrants from Naples and Campania in New York City, primarily in Brooklyn. Active from 1885 to 1918, they engaged in extortion, gambling, prostitution, and drug sales. Their links to Italian crime organizations were weak to non-existent.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Brooklyn Camorra or New York Camorra ( NY Camorra ) was a loose grouping of early-20th-century organized crime gangs that formed among Italian immigrants ... living in Greater New York, particularly in Brooklyn .
country_links.context: The Brooklyn Camorra operated in New York City, particularly Brooklyn, engaging in extortion, gambling, cocaine sales, and racketeering.
